hypothesis: Cross-cluster gradient coupling — when agent-optimization and system-health gradients fire simultaneously, their combined effect exceeds individual magnitudes. By routing tasks that activate both clusters (e.g., agent-file changes + system-health checks), we can amplify evolution signal and reduce redundant cycles.
research_question: Does co-activating agent-optimization and system-health gradient clusters produce multiplicative rather than additive evolution effects?
expected_outcome: Identify optimal task routing patterns that maximize cross-cluster gradient amplification
